title: Analysis of microplastics in drinking water and other clean water samples with micro-Raman and micro-infrared spectroscopy
methodology: Micro-Raman and micro-infrared (μ-IR) spectroscopy following harmonized sample preparation guidelines.
keyFindings: Established minimum requirements for microplastic analysis in clean water samples.
Identified critical particle size limits for Raman (1 μm) and IR (10-20 μm) spectroscopy.
Highlighted importance of blanks and contamination control in analytical labs.
Proposed a standard data reporting format for polymer types and size distributions.

title: AI-Driven Detection and Classification of Microplastics in Urban Water Systems
methodology: Deep Learning (CNN) combined with Automated Optical Microscopy for real-time monitoring.
keyFindings: Achieved 94% accuracy in classifying polymer types using lightweight CNN models.
Reduced processing time for large water samples from hours to minutes.
Observed higher microplastic densities near industrial discharge points in urban canals.
Developed an automated pipeline for size-based particle counting.

title: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence for Global Microplastics Monitoring Networks
methodology: Review of AI and Machine Learning applications in spectroscopic data analysis of microplastics.
keyFindings: AI significantly improves signal-to-noise ratios in Raman spectroscopy data.
Enables automated identification of weathered and biofilm-coated particles.
Machine learning models can predict the source of microplastics based on morphology.
Supports the creation of a global decentralized database for microplastics research.

title: Longitudinal Study of Microplastic Pollution in Indian Freshwater Systems
methodology: Seasonal sampling and FTIR analysis of surface water and sediments.
keyFindings: Seasonal variation shows 2.5x increase in microplastics during monsoon seasons.
Fragment and fiber morphology dominates urban stretches.
High correlation between population density and microplastic abundance.
Significant presence of secondary microplastics from textile washing.
